tweets.text:
People see brands doing $10M+ and think:
"wow the creative strategy is genius"

NOPE.

The real advantage is they:

Ship 50 creatives a week while u test 3
Killed 8 SKUs that weren't working
Spent $200k before u spent $20k

Boring answers usually win.
Speed beats strategy every

The ecom pipeline is genuinely broken

U spend:

$40 CPMs
Weeks testing creatives
Thousands on UGC creators

Just for some guy in Shenzhen to clone ur entire store in 48 hours and undercut u by 60%.

Then u wonder why your CAC doubled.

The internet created a generation of founders who are over-educated and under-executing.

They can explain CAC, LTV, ROAS, brand equity, viral loops, DTC supply chains, and Meta pixel optimization…

But can't:

Ship a product
Close a sale
Fire someone
Work past 6pm

They know
